key_points: Solving an equation means finding the value of the variable
The Balancing Method involves doing the same operation to both sides
Inverse Operations: Addition $\leftrightarrow$ Subtraction and Multiplication $\leftrightarrow$ Division
Transposition is the process of moving a term to the other side of the '=' sign
examples: Balancing: To solve $x - 3 = 7$, add 3 to both sides: $x - 3 + 3 = 7 + 3 \implies x = 10$
Check: If $x = 5$, then $4(5) = 20$. Since LHS = RHS, the answer is correct.
formulas: Balancing Rule $ightarrow$ LHS Operation = RHS Operation
Inverse Rule $ightarrow$ $+ ightarrow -$, $- ightarrow +$, $	imes ightarrow \div$, $\div ightarrow 	imes$
exam_tips: When transposing a number, always change its sign immediately
Always verify your answer by substituting it back into the original equation
Treat the '=' sign like a bridge that changes the nature of the number crossing it
Keep the variable on one side and all numbers on the other
